title: telemetry-processor component configuration
description: The configuration telemetry-processor itself understands: the object at `component.global` (checked here by `edgecommons component validate`), plus reusable `$defs` describing each `component.instances[]` entry (one ROUTE per instance — see `$defs/route`) for documentation and forward compatibility. The library envelope (logging, messaging, metricEmission, heartbeat, streaming, credentials, parameters, health, hierarchy, identity, tags, `component.token`) is owned by the canonical schema and is deliberately not redeclared here. `additionalProperties: false` throughout so a typo'd key is caught rather than silently ignored. See docs/reference/configuration.md for the authoritative field-by-field prose this schema was derived from.

defaults.description: Cross-route defaults under `component.global.defaults`, overlaid by each route (global ⊕ instance, instance wins per key).

key.description: Default aggregation / sample / stream-partition key path (a dotted key path — see `$defs/keyPath`).

target.description: Default `target` for a route that omits one: `local` | `northbound` | `stream:<name>`.

scriptsDir.description: Base directory for `{"file": "..."}` script references (template-resolved at startup). A relative script path resolves against it; an absolute path is used as-is. Defaults to the process working directory when unset.

scriptEngine.description: Default engine for every route's `filter`/`script` stages; a route's own `scriptEngine` overrides this.

instance.description: The shape of one `component.instances[]` entry. Tooling resolves instance config through this fixed name; the definition lives under its domain name below.

scriptEngine.description: `rhai` (pure-Rust, always compiled in) or `lua` (Lua 5.4 — needs the component's `scripting-lua` build feature).

keyPath.description: A dotted path over the message: roots `body.` (default when no known root prefix is present), `identity.`, `tags.`, `header.`; a `[]` suffix on a segment spreads across an array. Examples: `body.signal.id`, `body.samples[].quality`, `identity.device`.

route.title: One route — a component.instances[] entry
route.description: Each `component.instances[]` entry is one independent route: subscribe → pipeline → target. A malformed instance is logged and skipped at startup; startup fails only if zero routes build.

id.description: Route id — log-only identity, and (for `local` targets) the UNS `{instance}` token the dispatcher restamps output with. Unique among the component's routes.

subscribe.description: Topic filters on the local bus (MQTT `+`/`#` wildcards allowed). Each filter is template-resolved at startup. A filter shared by several routes is subscribed once and fanned out.

pipeline.description: Ordered processing stages. Empty = pass-through republisher.

target.description: Where the route's output goes. Required unless `global.defaults.target` is set. `local` (republish on the local bus), `northbound` (IoT Core / a northbound MQTT broker), or `stream:<name>` (a named entry under the top-level `streaming.streams[]`).

key.description: Route default key path for `sample`/`aggregate`/stream partitioning. Falls back to `global.defaults.key`, then `body.signal.id`.

maxQueue.description: Per-route internal queue depth (also the broker-side subscribe queue depth). Drop-on-full: when the route's worker can't keep up, new messages are dropped (tallied in `get-stats` `dropped`, surfaced as a rate-limited `evt/warning/queue-overflow`). Numeric fields accept an integer or an integer-valued float (Greengrass delivers config numbers as doubles).

scriptEngine.description: Engine for this route's `filter`/`script` stages. Falls back to `global.defaults.scriptEngine`, then `rhai`.

topic.description: Output topic template for `local`/`northbound` (default: the source topic). Template-resolved at startup. Must not resolve to a reserved UNS class (`state`/`metric`/`cfg`/`log`) and must not be combined with a script stage's own `output.topic`.

partitionKey.description: Partition-key path for `stream:<name>` targets (default: the route `key`, resolved per message).

stage.title: One pipeline stage
stage.description: An externally-tagged object: exactly one of `filter` / `sample` / `aggregate` / `project` / `script`.

filterStage.title: filter — keep/drop whole messages
filterStage.description: Exactly one form applies, checked in order: `script` → `quality` → `field`. A filter with none of the three fails the route at build time.

script.description: A boolean predicate (per the route's `scriptEngine`) over the message view; keep the message when it returns `true`.

quality.description: Shorthand: keep only when every `body.samples[].quality` equals this (and ≥1 sample exists).

field.description: Built-in predicate path (supports a `[]` array-spread segment → any-element match).

value.description: Right-hand value for the `field`/`op` comparison. Numbers and numeric strings compare numerically.

everyMs.description: Keep at most one message per key per this many ms.

everyN.description: Keep one in every N messages per key.

by.description: Per-key path. Falls back to the route `key`.

aggregateStage.title: aggregate — tumbling windowed reduction
aggregateStage.description: Emits one ProcessedTelemetry message per (key, window) on close. Time windows close on the worker flush tick or when a message for a newer window arrives; count windows close in-line when N is reached.

window.description: A time window (`"10s"` / `"500ms"`), or a bare record count (`"100"`).

by.description: Per-key path. Falls back to the route `key`.

fn.description: Reducers. The first listed is the primary (lands in `samples[0].value`).

value.description: Path to the value(s) to fold (a `[]` segment spreads an array). Defaults to every `body.samples[].value`, falling back to the whole body for a payload with no `samples`. Set this for a non-SouthboundSignalUpdate payload, e.g. `body.temperature`.

projectStage.title: project — reshape / whitelist the body
projectStage.description: With neither `keep` nor `set`, the body passes through unchanged.

keep.description: Body paths to retain. Only the first path segment (top-level body key) is kept — e.g. `"signal.id"` retains the whole `signal` object.

set.description: Literal fields overlaid onto the body.

scriptStage.title: script — a transform (Rhai or Lua)
scriptStage.description: Inline source (`{"script": "<source>"}`), the object form (`{"script": {"file"|"source": ..., "inputs": {...}, "output": {...}}}`, exactly one of file/source required), or the multi-signal object form carrying stateful `inputs` and/or an explicit `output`. All forms compile once at startup.

oneOf.description: Inline source. Good for a one-liner.

scriptSource.description: Inline script source, or `{"file": "<path>"}` read once at startup (relative to `global.defaults.scriptsDir`, or absolute).

scriptSpec.title: The object form of a script stage

file.description: Path to a `.rhai`/`.lua` file (relative to `global.defaults.scriptsDir`, or absolute), read once at startup. Exactly one of `file`/`source` is required.

source.description: Inline script source (the object-form equivalent of the bare string form). Exactly one of `file`/`source` is required.

inputs.description: Named multi-signal inputs: `{name: selector}`. Turns this into the stateful multi-signal stage — the stage caches the latest observation of every input and evaluates the script when a matched input's value or quality changes.

output.description: Publish each successful evaluation as a new EdgeCommons envelope on `output.topic` instead of mutating the triggering message in place.

inputSelector.title: A named script input's selector
inputSelector.description: At least one of `signalId`/`signalName`/`topic` is required; unknown selector fields fail the route at build time.

topic.description: Match the arriving topic against this MQTT-style filter (`+`/`#` supported).

device.description: Match the source envelope identity's device.

component.description: Match the source envelope identity's component token.

instance.description: Match the source envelope identity's instance token.

required.description: When `true`, the stage withholds every evaluation until this input has been observed. When `false` (default), the stage never waits on it.

topic.description: The output topic (template-resolved at startup). Must not be a reserved UNS class and must not match one of the route's own `subscribe` filters (a feedback loop).

name.description: The envelope header `name` of the derived message.

version.description: The envelope header `version` of the derived message.
